questions.prompt: Which design approach improves availability for a mission-critical application deployed on Amazon EC2?

options.text: Deploy instances in multiple Availability Zones behind a load balancer.
Deploy all instances in one Availability Zone with larger instance sizes.
Store application logs only on instance store volumes.
Use one NAT gateway as the primary failover target for application traffic.

questions.explanation: Spreading EC2 capacity across Availability Zones behind a load balancer reduces the impact of an Availability Zone failure.

questions.prompt: Which two controls can help secure access to AWS resources in a multi-account environment?

options.text: Apply service control policies in AWS Organizations.
Use IAM roles with least privilege permissions.
Share the root user credentials with platform teams.

metadata.explanation: Service control policies and least privilege IAM roles are standard controls for governing access across multiple AWS accounts.
